food_name: Lontong Sayur Lodeh (Compressed Rice Cake in Vegetable Coconut Milk Gravy)
food_subgroup: Compressed rice cake (lontong) served in vegetable coconut milk gravy (sayur lodeh); 1 portion approximately 350g

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn does not have a specific entry for Lontong Sayur Lodeh. No exact FoodOn URI exists for this Malay/Javanese compressed rice cake preparation. Closest parent would be a rice-based mixed dish, but no defensible code is available.
ontology.foodex2_description: Rice-based composite dish (closest parent: mixed rice dish with vegetables and legumes in coconut milk sauce). FoodEx2 does not have a specific code for lontong sayur lodeh; A000E (composite rice dish) is the closest defensible parent.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: No specific FoodEx2 code exists for lontong sayur lodeh. A000E used as closest defensible parent for a composite rice-based dish.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code exists for this specific Malay rice cake preparation.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving of lontong sayur lodeh is approximately 350g, comprising compressed rice cake pieces and a generous ladle of vegetable coconut milk gravy with tofu, tempeh, and vegetables.

nutrient_source.title: Lontong with Sayur Lodeh — Health Promotion Board Singapore (HPB) / IMR Malaysia MyFCD
nutrient_source.organization: Health Promotion Board Singapore (HPB) / Institute for Medical Research Malaysia (IMR) MyFCD
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced from HPB Singapore food composition data for lontong sayur lodeh and cross-referenced with IMR Malaysia MyFCD. Values represent a composite dish of compressed rice cake and sayur lodeh vegetable coconut milk gravy. Per-100g values are consistent with HPB published data for this dish category. Copper, manganese, and vitamin K values are estimated from component-level data where direct dish-level data is unavailable.

gi_source.title: Glycemic Index of Rice-Based Foods — University of Sydney GI Database; Atkinson FS et al. (2021) International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values 2021, Diabetes Care
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care (peer-reviewed journal)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for lontong (compressed rice cake in sayur lodeh) in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI of 75 is estimated by analogy with white rice cake / compressed rice products reported in the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. (2021). Compression of rice increases starch density relative to regular cooked white rice (GI ~72), supporting a GI estimate of ~75 for lontong. Glycemic load per 350g serving calculated as: (75 × 65.45g available carbohydrate) / 100 = 49.1. The sayur lodeh vegetables, tempeh, and tofu provide fibre and protein that partially moderate the postprandial glucose response but do not substantially lower the GI of the rice cake component.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 560mg per 350g serving (160mg/100g), primarily from dried shrimp and cooking salt in sayur lodeh
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit lontong sayur lodeh to one small portion and request reduced salt and dried shrimp in the gravy, as a single 350g serving provides approximately 24% of the 2300mg daily sodium limit recommended by HPB Singapore.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Very high glycemic load (~49.1 per 350g serving) due to compressed rice cake with GI ~75 and 65.5g carbohydrate per portion
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should significantly reduce the lontong rice cake portion to one piece (~80–100g) and increase the sayur lodeh vegetable and tempeh component, as the full 350g serving delivers a glycemic load of approximately 49 — well above the recommended per-meal GL target of 20 or less; at Hari Raya, eating the vegetable gravy first before limiting rice cake intake is a practical harm-reduction strategy.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat 10.5g per 350g serving from coconut milk (predominantly medium-chain triglycerides); sodium 560mg per serving
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should consume lontong sayur lodeh in moderation, as the coconut milk contributes approximately 10.5g saturated fat per serving (53% of the 20g daily limit); however, the MCT-dominant saturated fat profile of coconut milk and the anti-inflammatory isoflavones from tempeh partially offset cardiovascular risk compared with animal-fat sources.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Tempeh isoflavones (genistein, daidzein) with documented anti-inflammatory properties; diverse anti-inflammatory vegetables
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Lontong sayur lodeh is generally suitable for individuals with rheumatoid arthritis as the tempeh provides anti-inflammatory isoflavones and the vegetable-rich sayur lodeh supplies phytonutrients; the primary concern is the saturated fat from coconut milk, which should be moderated.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Lontong Sayur Lodeh is a traditional Malay and Javanese dish widely consumed in Singapore, Malaysia, and Indonesia. It consists of lontong — compressed white rice cakes made by boiling raw rice tightly packed inside a banana leaf or plastic cylinder until the grains fuse into a dense, sliceable block — served in sayur lodeh, a mildly spiced vegetable stew cooked in coconut milk. The sayur lodeh gravy is flavoured with a spice paste of turmeric, galangal, lemongrass, and shallots, and contains a variety of vegetables including cabbage, long beans, carrot, chayote, and eggplant, along with firm tofu, tempeh, and dried shrimp. The dish is often garnished with sambal chili paste, a hard-boiled egg, and serunding (spiced coconut flakes). The texture of lontong is firm and chewy, contrasting with the rich, golden, aromatic coconut milk broth. Nutritionally, lontong sayur lodeh provides 130 kcal per 100g, with 4g protein, 4g fat (3g saturated, predominantly from coconut milk MCTs), and 18.7g carbohydrate. A standard 350g serving delivers approximately 455 kcal and a very high glycemic load of ~49, making it a significant carbohydrate challenge for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes. The tempeh contributes complete plant protein and anti-inflammatory isoflavones; the diverse vegetables provide dietary fibre, vitamin C, and phytonutrients. Lontong is a quintessential Hari Raya Aidilfitri dish in Singapore's Malay community and is also available daily at Malay hawker stalls. Clinical advice for T2DM management: limit rice cake to one piece and prioritise the vegetable and tempeh components of the sayur lodeh.
common_user_queries: lontong sayur lodeh calories per serving

confidence_reason: Core nutrient values sourced from HPB Singapore food composition data and cross-referenced with IMR Malaysia MyFCD for lontong sayur lodeh as a composite dish. GI estimated by analogy with compressed rice cake products in the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. (2021), which is the closest defensible approved GI source. Copper, manganese, and vitamin K values are component-level estimates with confidence 0.9. Monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, and water content are not reported in the primary source and are set to -1.
notes: 1. Monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g are set to -1: detailed fatty acid fractionation data for lontong sayur lodeh as a composite dish is not available in HPB Singapore or IMR Malaysia MyFCD databases as of 2026. 2. Water_g is set to -1: moisture content for this composite dish is not reported in the primary sources consulted. 3. Trans_fat_g is 0.0 — confirmed negligible/zero per HPB Singapore data for this dish; no hydrogenated fats are used in traditional preparation. 4. Vitamin_d_mcg is 0.0 — confirmed negligible per HPB Singapore data; this is a plant-dominant dish with no significant vitamin D sources. 5. Copper (0.1 mg/100g), manganese (0.3 mg/100g), and vitamin K (12.0 mcg/100g) are estimated from component-level data (vegetables, tempeh, coconut milk) where direct dish-level data is unavailable in approved sources; confidence 0.9. 6. GI of 75 is an analogous estimate based on compressed rice cake products in the University of Sydney GI Database; no direct GI measurement for lontong exists in the database. GL per 350g serving = (75 × 65.45) / 100 = 49.1. 7. The food_group has been corrected from 'Malay Heritage Kuih & Desserts' (input) to 'Mixed' as lontong sayur lodeh is a savoury main dish, not a dessert or kuih. 8. FoodEx2 code A000E used as closest defensible parent for a composite rice-based dish; no specific FoodEx2 code exists for lontong sayur lodeh.
